items.name: The Columns & Francis Quadrangle
items.description: Six iconic Ionic limestone columns — all that remained after Academic Hall burned in 1892. The heart of campus and site of Tiger Walk, graduation photos, and countless Mizzou memories.

items.name: Jesse Hall
items.description: The main administrative building directly behind the Columns. Home to the Chancellor's office, large auditoriums, and lecture halls. The dome is a campus landmark.

items.name: Ellis Library
items.description: MU's main library with millions of volumes, individual and group study rooms, and research assistance. Extended hours during finals week. Bookmark Café is inside.

items.name: Memorial Union
items.description: Central student hub with dining (Starbucks, Tiger Den food court), meeting rooms, the campus bookstore, and student organization offices. Tip your hat or whisper when passing through the arches — a Mizzou tradition honoring fallen soldiers.

items.name: MU Student Center
items.description: Houses the Center for Student Involvement, Student Health & Well-Being offices (G202), Mizzou Market – Central, bowling lanes, and student organization space. One of the most-visited buildings on campus.

items.name: Memorial Stadium (Faurot Field)
items.description: Home of Mizzou Tigers football and the Antlers student section. Capacity ~71,000. Also the traditional site for Painting the Rock M during Welcome Week.

items.name: Mizzou Arena
items.description: Home of Mizzou Tigers men's and women's basketball. Also hosts concerts, convocations, and major campus events like First Roar during Welcome Week.

items.name: MizzouRec (Student Recreation Complex)
items.description: Free for enrolled students. Includes cardio and weight rooms, indoor pools, a climbing wall, basketball courts, racquetball courts, group fitness classes, and outdoor equipment rentals.

items.name: Student Health Center
items.description: Primary care and urgent care for MU students at 909 Hitt St. Same- or next-day appointments almost always available. Call 573-882-7481.

items.name: Mark Twain Hall
items.description: Park View neighborhood residence hall at 515 S. 5th St. The MARK on 5th Street all-you-care-to-eat dining hall is inside. Close to the College of Engineering and Peace Park.

items.name: Wolpers Hall
items.description: Truman Central neighborhood residence hall at 800 Hitt St. Across from MizzouRec. Central campus location close to the Student Center and Student Health Center.

items.name: Plaza 900 Dining Hall
items.description: The largest all-you-care-to-eat dining hall on campus at 900 Virginia Ave. Features the largest salad bar on campus, a grill, pizza, and Central Dairy ice cream. Mizzou Meals accepted.

items.name: Restaurants at Southwest
items.description: À la carte food hall at 502 Kentucky Blvd with six restaurants: Legacy Grill, Olive + Oil, 1•5•3 Salads & Soup, Tiger Deli, and 1839 Kitchen.

items.name: Peace Park
items.description: A four-acre wooded retreat on the north edge of campus with the McAlester Bridge. A quiet escape for studying, relaxing, or taking a break from campus life.

items.name: Strickland Hall (Counseling Center)
items.description: The MU Counseling Center is on the 4th floor. Free mental health services for enrolled students. Crisis line available 24/7 at 573-882-6601.
